Financial Performance

Step-by-step savings calculation from historical benchmark to earned savings/losses.

Step Description Value MSSP Median Peer Median
1 Assigned Beneficiaries 11,048 p38 13,159 9,942
2 Historical Benchmark (per capita) $9,639 p6 $11,439 $10,652
3 Regional Adjustment +$77
4 Prior Savings Adjustment +-
5 Adjustment Category Regional Adjustment
6 Updated Benchmark (per capita) $11,866 p18 $13,279 $12,265
7 Total Benchmark (all beneficiaries) $128,476,577
8 Total Expenditure (all beneficiaries) $122,384,135
9 Per Capita Expenditure (PY) $11,303 p19 $12,693 $11,726
10 Generated Savings / Losses $6,092,443 $6,931,306 —
11 Savings Rate 4.74% p54 4.21% 3.44%
12 Minimum Savings Rate (threshold) 2.94%
13 Guardrail Applied —
14 Final Shared Savings Rate 29.37%
15 Final Shared Loss Rate -
16 Earned Savings / Losses $1,753,683 $3,685,876 —
Revenue-to-Expense Category High Revenue

Annual per-beneficiary expenditure across benchmark years (BY1–BY3) and the performance year (PY). Includes Parts A and B FFS spending.

Population Segment BY1BY2BY3PY Trend Risk-Adj. Expected Difference

Total $11,303 $12,693 $11,726

Risk Scores

Hierarchical Condition Category risk scores. A score of 1.0 = national average. Below 1.0 means the population is healthier than average.

CMS-HCC Risk Scores

Population Segment BY1BY2BY3PY Trend Coding Gap MSSP Med.
End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) 1.004 0.992 0.945 0.910 ↓ -0.037 0.967
Disabled 0.888 0.931 0.895 0.907 → -0.073 0.934
Aged, Dual-Eligible 0.844 0.816 0.853 0.812 ↓ -0.202 0.954
Aged, Non-Dual 0.858 0.849 0.842 0.849 → -0.127 0.983

Demographic Risk Scores

Demographic-only risk scores (age/sex/Medicaid status). Compared with HCC risk scores, the difference reflects clinical coding intensity.

Population Segment BY3PY
End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) — 0.947
Disabled — 0.980
Aged, Dual-Eligible — 1.014
Aged, Non-Dual — 0.976

Risk Redistribution Weights (PY)

Fraction of the ACO's total risk attributable to each population segment.

ESRD Weight 2.0%
Disabled Weight 5.7%
Aged Dual Weight 3.6%
Aged Non-Dual Weight 88.6%

Clinical Quality Measures

MSSP quality measures. Measures marked "lower is better" are inverted — a lower score indicates better performance.

Measure This ACO MSSP Median Peer Median
Overall Quality Score (Composite) p11 73.43 83.13 79.42
All-Cause Unplanned Admissions (risk-adjusted rate) (lower is better) ? p81 0.16 0.15 0.15
Risk-Standardized All-Condition Readmission Rate (lower is better) ? p76 40.85 36.11 33.97
Falls: Screening for Future Fall Risk p32 88.13 91.53 90.56
Influenza Immunization p33 64.11 70.06 65.14

Statin Therapy for CVD Prevention p33 85.48 87.36 86.70
Depression Remission at 12 Months p90 32.43 14.29 14.81

✗ Met Quality Performance Standard (QPS)
✓ Met Alternative QPS
✗ Met 40th Percentile Threshold

Peer Cohort

30 similar small, BASIC-track, average acuity, hospital-affiliated ACOs

View 30 peer ACOs
A4984A2599A3664A5399A3555A3698A2202A5334A1737A2189A5403A5402A3722A4943A5374A2083A5169A3694A2528A3723A3873A5438A3731A5058A5328A4613A2070A5067A2706A3623

Peers selected using weighted distance across 15 structural features (population acuity, composition, scale, care infrastructure, assignment type) within the BASIC track partition. Learn more

Methodology

Data source: MSSP ACO Performance PY2024 (dataset ID: mssp-performance). Vintage: PY2024. Downloaded: 2026-04-12. Rows in source: 476.

MSSP ACO Performance reports financial and quality performance for Accountable Care Organizations participating in the Medicare Shared Savings Program. Savings/losses are calculated against risk-adjusted, regionally-weighted benchmarks.

Full methodology →

MIT License. Free. No ads. No login.
GitHub · Report an error
